query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "gatemem: benchmarking memory governance in multi-principal shared-memo" computer science
rationale: This paper directly addresses the user's question by providing the canonical follow-up work on "memory governance in multi-principal shared memory" for LLMs, satisfying criteria (a) and (b) as it measures the same biological/algorithmic mechanism (governance primitives like leakage, stale propagation, and provenance) in the exact same domain (multi-agent LLM systems). It effectively serves as the primary related work for a study extending the "GateMem" benchmark.
